pitchbook.content: Gymshark (legal name: Gymshark Limited) is a Solihull, England, United Kingdom-based company founded in 2012. Manufacturer of fitness apparel and accessories intended to serve athletes, gym-goers, and sports enthusiasts. The company designs and markets jackets, tracksuits, bags, and customized apparel for both men and women. Gymshark operates in the Consumer Products and Services (B2C) sector within the Apparel and Accessories group (industry code: Clothing). The company is currently Generating Revenue with Privately Held and Private Equity-Backed (as of 09/19/2023). Gymshark employs 1000 people (as of 10/24/2025) and reported 577.41574007 in revenue for TTM 4Q2024. Website: www.gymshark.com. HQ: GSHQ Blythe Valley Business Park, 3 Central Boulevard, Solihull, England, B90 8AB.
pitchbook.title: Gymshark Company Profile

pitchbook.content: Gymshark has raised 251.11053635 million USD in total funding. The most recent financing was a Debt - General of 251.11053635 closed on 09/19/2023. In 09/16/2020, Gymshark raised in a PE Growth/Expansion round with a post-money valuation of 1312.60582884 (Estimated). Revenue at time of deal was 328.60276317. In 10/12/2021, Gymshark raised in an IPO round. Revenue at time of deal was 592.95305184. In 09/19/2023, Gymshark raised 251.11053635 in a Debt - General round. Revenue at time of deal was 488.4343702. Current Investors: General Atlantic (www.generalatlantic.com) has been an investor since 09/16/2020, Status: Active, Holding: Minority. Similar Companies: Outdoor Voices, Myles Athletic, Eddie Bauer, Reebok, prAna, Ten Thousand, J.Crew Group, Lands End Europe, Ann Taylor, Fabletics.
pitchbook.title: Gymshark Funding History & Competitors

pitchbook.content: Gymshark Group (legal name: Gymshark Group Limited) is a Solihull, England, United Kingdom-based company founded in 2020. Operates in the Financial Services sector (Holding Companies). Gymshark Group employs 881 people (as of 12/31/2024) and reported 764.65811351 in revenue for TTM 4Q2024. HQ: Gshq Blythe Valley Park 3 Central Boulevard, Solihull, England, B90 8AB.

statista.content: Global sports apparel revenue forecast 2023-2030: The global sports apparel market was estimated to generate around 213 billion U.S. dollars in revenue in 2023. Revenues are forecast to increase and reach around 294 billion U.S. dollars in 2030. Nike and adidas were the leading sportswear companies worldwide in 2022, generating combined revenues of over 70 billion U.S. dollars. Lululemon net revenue exceeded 8.1 billion U.S. dollars in 2022, increasing by around 30 percent on the previous year.
statista.title: Global sports apparel revenue forecast 2023-2030

statista.content: Retail sales of the global sportswear and outdoor apparel market 2012-2021: In 2016, the global sportswear and outdoor apparel market generated an estimated 172.3 billion U.S. dollars in retail sales. The global apparel market was forecast to be worth a total of around 1.5 trillion U.S. dollars by 2020. The global outdoor clothing market was valued at approximately 12.7 billion U.S. dollars in 2018, and forecast to reach 19.6 billion by 2026.
statista.title: Retail sales of the global sportswear and outdoor apparel market 2012-2021

statista.content: Global athletic apparel market share 2015: Adidas held 11.6 percent share of the global athletic apparel market, ahead of Nike at 10.8 percent. VF Corp, Under Armour, and Gildan were top five. Nike generates more revenue from footwear than adidas and Puma combined. Nike dominates the U.S. athletic footwear market with over half market share.
statista.title: Global athletic apparel market share (Nike, Adidas, etc.) 2015

statista.content: Revenue of leading sportswear companies worldwide FY2023: Nike generated the most revenues with approximately 51.2 billion dollars. Other major players include Adidas, Puma, VF Corporation, Lululemon, Under Armour, and Mizuno.
statista.title: Revenue of leading sportswear companies worldwide 2023

statista.content: Sports apparel market share by company U.S. 2018: Nike held 18.3 percent share of the athletic apparel market in the United States, ahead of adidas at six percent.
statista.title: Sports apparel market share by company U.S. 2018

statista.content: Forecast athletic leisure apparel market Canada 2024: Market value stood at roughly 3.7 billion Canadian dollars in 2020, forecast to reach nearly five billion Canadian dollars by 2024.
statista.title: Forecast athletic leisure apparel market Canada 2024

statista.content: Direct-to-consumer (D2C) e-commerce revenue in Germany declined from 3.4 billion euros in 2021 to 2.8 billion euros in 2024.
statista.title: Germany: direct-to-consumer ecommerce revenue 2024

cbinsights.content: Ben Francis founded Gymshark in 2012 in the UK when he was just 19. Unhappy with existing sportswear choices, he wanted to build a clothing brand that offered affordable and fashionable products to gym-goers. To launch, Francis turned to popular social media accounts in fitness and health niches, making Gymshark one of the pioneers of influencer marketing. The company initially contacted high-profile accounts such as bodybuilders Lex Griffin, Chris Lavado, and Nikki Blackketter. Gymshark now sponsors 18 influencers, including Irish professional boxer Katie Taylor and ultra-marathon swimmer Ross Edgley. Gymshark uses influencers to bond with gym enthusiasts and deepens brand loyalty by creating exclusivity — selling products exclusively through its website. In 2020, US fund manager General Atlantic invested over $250M into Gymshark, valuing the company at $1.3B. The funds will be used to expand into North America and Asia.
cbinsights.title: 22 Biggest Direct-To-Consumer Success Stories — Gymshark Analysis

cbinsights.content: Unbundling Nike: How Direct-To-Consumer Retail Is Being Disrupted. Nike has grown its direct-to-consumer sales to 39% of its Nike brand revenues in 2021, up from 16%.
cbinsights.title: Unbundling Nike: How Direct-To-Consumer Retail Is Being Disrupted

cbinsights.content: Consumer Goods M&A Trends: In 2016, corporations led 71% of all consumer startup acquisitions, up from 23% in 2007. Private equity firms accounted for just 19% of acquisitions. Apparel & accessories consistently ranked second in M&A activity behind food & beverage. The global influencer marketing platform market is expected to reach $24.1B by 2025, growing at 32% CAGR.
cbinsights.title: Consumer Goods M&A Trends
